message:
fix(python): masked_invalid must construct MaskedArray via ma.array(a, mask=)

Correct the masked_invalid wrapper landed in the previous parity
commit so it produces a `numpy.ma.MaskedArray` with the expected
shape and dtype, not just a view of the underlying data passed
through `masked_where`.

crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs:
- Switch from `numpy.ma.masked_where(invalid, a, copy=copy)` to
  `numpy.ma.array(a, mask=invalid, copy=copy)`. masked_where returns
  a view semantically tied to the boolean mask argument, which breaks
  downstream dtype/shape expectations for array-inputs that are not
  plain ndarrays (structured arrays, subclasses). `ma.array(...)` is
  the canonical constructor NumPy itself uses internally for
  masked_invalid and preserves dtype/shape for every supported input.
- Set the `mask` kwarg alongside `copy` in the PyDict so both are
  forwarded in one call; the order matches NumPy's documented API.
- Small test cleanup: construct the `object_values` PyList via
  PyList::new with an explicit element slice instead of append(),
  which is the pyo3-recommended pattern after the bound-ref upgrades
  (PyList::empty + append is a legacy-style idiom) and keeps the
  test free of deprecation warnings on newer pyo3 releases.

No beads state changes in this commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
